title: The Norton Anthology of Theory and Criticism
excerpt: The most comprehensive anthology of literary theory and criticism in the English language, spanning from Plato to the digital age with authoritative introductions and bibliographies.
keyIdeas: Theory and criticism span a continuous tradition from classical antiquity to the present
Every theoretical position emerges in dialogue with earlier traditions
Canonical texts must be read in their historical and intellectual contexts
Contemporary theory is global in scope, not limited to Western traditions
keyTakeaways: Access the complete intellectual history of literary theory in one volume
Understand the conversation between different theoretical traditions across centuries
Identify the key texts that have shaped modern critical practice
whoShouldRead: Graduate students and scholars in literary and cultural studies
Anyone who wants a comprehensive reference for theoretical texts
whoShouldSkip: Readers seeking a simplified introduction to theory
Those who prefer shorter, more curated selections
